Webpack bundler config fix — internal build system, no user-facing docs impact.
Internal admin panel crash fix (race condition in permission loading) — no new features or config changes.
Router: Internal content-manager UI bug fix (event.preventDefault). Single-line fix for browser default behavior. No user-facing behavior change or API modification.

Router: Internal content-manager UI bug fix for drag-and-drop preview behavior. The fix ensures the preview doesn't disappear during drag operations. This is a visual bug fix with no user-facing API or feature changes.

Router: Bug fix in the OpenAPI generation plugin. The fix corrects the plugin to generate parameter types matching the actual API specification (documentId as string, not number). The REST API documentation at cms/api/rest already correctly documents IDs as strings. This is an internal plugin fix with no new user-facing behavior requiring docs updates.

Router: Bug fix to preserve self-referential relations during publish/discard operations. The existing Draft & Publish documentation at cms/features/draft-and-publish.md already covers the feature. This fix corrects the implementation to match the documented behavior (relations should be preserved). No new user-facing features or API changes require documentation updates.

Router: Internal database migration fix with no change to user-facing behavior, APIs, or configuration. The idempotency improvements and error handling are implementation details of the internal 5.0.0-02-created-document-id migration that don't require user documentation.

Router: Internal bug fix for review workflows. The fix ensures stage/assignee selection correctly respects locale params in i18n-enabled content types. No new functionality or API changes - the behavior aligns with what's already documented. Documentation does not need updates.

Router: Internal admin UI styling fix that addresses a rendering bug (double scrollbar in list view). This is an implementation detail of the OverflowingItem styled component and does not introduce new user-facing features, APIs, or behavior changes. Users do not interact with or configure this component directly. No documentation update needed.

Router: Internal bug fix in content manager crash handling when viewing history of entries with deleted dynamic zone components. No public API change, no new user-facing behavior — just fixes an unexpected crash.
Triage: admin UI polish with no behavior change; removes spurious console warnings
Router: Internal framework migration from Yup to Zod for server-side validation schemas. This is implementation detail, not a user-facing API or configuration change.
Router: Internal bug fix in URL parameter serialization that prevents trailing ? in query strings. Fixes a side effect (duplicate history versions) but doesn't change public API surface.
Router: UI enhancement to the Review Workflows admin panel feature (incremental loading + search in assignee dropdown). No API change or configuration change.
Router: Internal TypeScript type fix for admin router component. No user-facing API or behavior change, no public documentation impact.
Router: Internal database optimization/fix for PostgreSQL deadlock prevention. No user-facing API or behavior change, no documentation needed.
Triage: internal refactoring — no behavior change expected
Triage: test infrastructure/flakiness fix

Router: Internal Document Service API bug fix for x-to-one relation handling with Draft & Publish. Fixes an internal behavior issue where draft and published entries weren't being linked correctly. No public API changes or user-facing behavior changes; users should not need to change their code.

Router: UI/CSS fix in Content Manager's Dynamic Zone component picker. Addresses a visual overflow issue where long component names without spaces would bleed into adjacent cards. This is a visual/UX improvement with no impact on documented behavior, features, or APIs.
Router: Internal admin UI bug fix for Prism.js library loading issue in Code block component. No public API or user-facing behavior change.

Router: Database query helper bug fix for morph relation population. Internal correctness fix ensuring morphTo relations return correct type discriminator; not a new feature or breaking change requiring user documentation.
Router: Admin UI bug fix for filter persistence in Content Manager. Fixes stale localStorage data after clearing filters. Internal UX improvement with no public API change.
Triage: internal schema hashing optimization

Router: Internal bug fix in query parameter validation. Throws correct HTTP status code (400 Bad Request instead of 500) for invalid user input — no public API surface change. Error messages remain unchanged and user-facing behavior is already documented.
Router: Bug fix in admin fetch client's response handling (Safari error on 204 No Content). No public API or user-facing behavior change — entirely internal to admin UI error handling.
Triage: performance optimization that skips expensive queries yielding identical results; no user-facing behavior, API, or feature changes
Triage: internal optimization to telemetry calls

Router: Internal database query builder fix. Adds a stable sort order to paginated selects to prevent duplicate/skipped rows, but this is an implementation detail of the query builder. No user-facing API or behavior change that requires documentation; developers don't interact with this change directly.
Router: Internal development tool fix for package manager detection. No user-facing API or documentation changes needed.
Router: Internal admin UI bug fix. No user-facing feature changes or documentation updates needed.
Router: Internal admin UI improvement fixing date picker max year constraint. The date picker is a technical implementation detail of the admin panel, not a user-facing content feature.
Router: Internal bug fix for transaction handling in Content Releases. Fixes serialization of concurrent publishes. No new features or API changes affecting documentation.

Router: Internal admin UI bug fix. Fixes a crash in component configuration layout conversion when handling nested components. No new public APIs, features, or user-facing behavior changes. Marked as internal refactor fixing issue #25509.
Triage: Internal migration code fix; doesn't change user-facing behavior, APIs, configuration, or features

Router: Internal utilities/upgrade package fix. Aligns devDependencies during the upgrade process. Does not affect any public-facing APIs or user-facing behavior that would require documentation updates.

Router: Enterprise Edition feature. Fixes a UX issue in the Admin panel when switching Review Workflows locales. The change is an internal error handling improvement (returning 200 with empty data instead of 404, showing a clearer message to users). This is a bug fix/UX improvement that doesn't require documentation changes—the feature behavior remains the same, just with better error messages.
Triage: dependency maintenance and security patches without behavior change
Router: Internal deprecation warning fix. Users do not interact with this — it silences false positive warnings on existing projects. No user-facing behavior or configuration changes to document.

Router: Internal admin panel bug fix that improves UX of existing session timeout feature. No new user-facing capabilities, configuration options, or APIs introduced. Session management should already be documented if needed.

Router: Internal bug fix aligning server-side validator with already-documented public API. No user-facing behavior or API contract changes. The documentation already correctly references 'language' as the code block field.
Router: Internal database migration optimization (batching improvements). No public API, feature, or user-facing behavior changes. This is implementation-level performance tuning.
Router: Bug fix for PR #25793 regression. Once #25793 is documented, this fix is already covered by that documentation. This is implementation-level, no new user-facing behavior.
